composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in app.py
- Develop the main Flask application accessible on local port 5000, with the first page at route '/'.
- Implement routing for all pages  dashboard (/dashboard), create article (/article/create), edit article (/article/<article_id>/edit), version history (/article/<article_id>/versions), my articles (/articles/mine), published articles (/articles/published), content calendar (/calendar), and article analytics (/article/<article_id>/analytics).
- Implement file read/write operations for all data files in the 'data' directory (users.txt, articles.txt, article_versions.txt, approvals.txt, workflow_stages.txt, comments.txt, analytics.txt) using plain text starting from the first line.
- Implement core features  version control, content scheduling, analytics aggregation, collaborative content creation with version history tracking, editorial comments.
- Manage user sessions/authentication (basic or placeholder).
- Ensure all routes correspond to frontend pages.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Complete the frontend code for all pages  dashboard.html, create_article.html, edit_article.html, version_history.html, my_articles.html, published_articles.html, content_calendar.html, article_analytics.html.
- Use the specified element IDs for frontend interaction and testing.
- All URLs in the frontend code should be Flask routes, not direct HTML file names.
- Ensure the frontend supports the features defined in the specification, including content overview, article creation/editing, version history viewing/restoring, article listing with filters, public content library, content scheduling calendar, and article analytics display.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory
- Implement CSS styles in css/styles.css to style the web application consistently and responsively.
- Implement JavaScript in js/scripts.js for UI interactions, form validations, and AJAX calls if needed to enhance user experience.
- Organize and include featured images and other media assets in img/ as referenced by articles.
- Ensure static assets support the frontend templates and provide smooth, interactive user experience.
